Patricia, the chart looks great. Can you walk me through the main trend?
Of course. The score increased sharply and reached a peak in month four, then it leveled off into a plateau.
And the red line? It looks like the opposite.
Exactly. The control group shows a slow decline, so the treatment line is much higher than the control.
One thing: please label the vertical axis. It is not obvious what the numbers mean.
Good point. I will label the axis and highlight the proportion of responders at the top.
The horizontal axis shows the months and the vertical axis shows the score.
The overall trend is clearly positive.
It rose sharply to a peak and then reached a plateau.
The treatment group is much higher than the control.
A high proportion of patients responded well.

Good morning, everyone. Let me walk you through the key results of our six-month clinical study. If you look at the first chart, the horizontal axis shows the months, and the vertical axis shows the average score for each group. Overall, the trend is very encouraging. In the first two months, we saw a steady increase in the main marker, and then it reached a clear peak in month four. After that, the values did not collapse. Instead, they reached a plateau and stayed almost flat until the end. The control group, on the other hand, showed a slow decline over the same period. One number I want to highlight is the proportion of patients who responded well: it rose from about forty percent to nearly seventy percent. The figures did fluctuate a little from week to week, which is normal, but the overall direction is clear and positive. So, to summarize, the data shows a sharp early increase, a stable plateau, and a much higher proportion of responders than in the control group.

Hi Patricia, this is David from the data team. I had a chance to review the charts you sent for the trends presentation, and they look really clear. I like how the line graph shows the sharp increase in the first quarter and then the plateau. Just two small comments. First, on the second slide, please label the vertical axis, because right now it is not obvious what the numbers mean. Second, the decline in the control group is important, so maybe make that line a little bolder so it stands out. The proportion figures are a strong point, so I would put them near the top. Overall, the data tells a convincing story. Let us talk tomorrow and finalize the slides before the meeting. Thanks again, and great work on the analysis.
